seoTitle: How to Delete a Jira Site
metaDescription: Learn how to permanently delete an entire Jira site, including what to back up first since the process removes all associated data.
intro: Deleting a Jira site is a significant, permanent action that removes all associated projects, issues, and data. This guide covers what to check before deleting a site and how to complete the process.

steps.heading: Confirm this is the right action
steps.body: Deleting a site removes everything on it permanently, so make sure you don't simply need to remove a single project or deactivate users instead.

steps.heading: Back up any data you need
steps.body: Export or archive any issues, reports, or attachments you might need later, since none of it will be recoverable after deletion.

steps.heading: Notify your team
steps.body: Let anyone using the site know it's being deleted, since they'll lose access to all projects and data once it's gone.

steps.heading: Access site administration settings
steps.body: Go to your organization's site administration area, which is where site-level actions like deletion are managed.

steps.heading: Confirm the deletion
steps.body: Follow the prompts to permanently delete the site, which typically requires a final confirmation given how significant the action is.

faq.question: Can a deleted Jira site be restored?
faq.answer: In most cases deletion is permanent, though some plans may offer a limited recovery window, so check Atlassian's current policy before proceeding.

faq.question: Who has permission to delete a Jira site?
faq.answer: This is typically restricted to organization or site administrators, given how significant the action is.

faq.question: Should I delete a site or just a project?
faq.answer: If you only need to remove one project's data, deleting the project is the safer, more targeted option rather than removing the entire site.

faq.question: What happens to billing after a site is deleted?
faq.answer: Deleting a site generally affects associated subscriptions, so review your billing settings to confirm changes take effect as expected.
